Title: A cellulose synthesis inhibitor affects cellulose synthase complex secretion and cortical microtubule dynamics

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GFP-KOR1 was used as a validated proxy for cellulose synthase complex (CSC) behavior because KOR1 is part of the CSC complex. 2. In the kor1 mutant background, P4B reduced plasma membrane CSC particle density and altered trajectory orientation without changing particle velocity after 5 h. 3. In the kor1/cesa3pbr1 background, these P4B effects were bypassed, supporting CESA3-dependent control of CSC delivery/organization.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reports CSC density/orientation changes under P4B treatment; no direct causal gene manipulation on cellulose content in this study.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major structural polysaccharide required for cell wall assembly and anisotropic cell elongation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Live-cell imaging of GFP-KOR1 particle density, velocity, and trajectory orientation in kor1lt and kor1lt/cesa3pbr1 backgrounds
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: P4B treatment (5 h and 24 h), dark-grown hypocotyl epidermis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core/common gene because it was experimentally central for tracking CSC trafficking/behavior, but it is not the principal causal target gene for the cellulose phenotype.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct value; useful as a marker/CSC-associated component for dissecting cellulose biosynthesis inhibitor responses.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A novel P4B-resistant mutant (pbr1) was mapped to CESA3, causing an L883F substitution in the fourth transmembrane domain. 2. Complementation with pCESA3-CESA3 restored P4B sensitivity, confirming CESA3 as causative for resistance. 3. Independent isoxaben-resistant cesa3 alleles (ixr1-1, ixr1-2) also conferred cross-tolerance to P4B, whereas cesa6ixr2-1 did not. 4. P4B inhibited cellulose accumulation and 13C-glucose incorporation into crystalline cellulose in wild type but not in cesa3pbr1. 5. Short-term P4B reduced CSC delivery to the plasma membrane and altered CSC trajectory orientation without reducing CSC velocity, and cesa3pbr1 bypassed these effects.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper suggests P4B may directly target CESA3 or affect another function linked to CSC plasma membrane delivery and/or microtubule dynamics that is specifically bypassed by CESA3 mutations. Resistance-conferring mutations mapped to TM4, TM7, and the gating loop.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: cesa3pbr1 had thinner walls even without P4B, indicating reduced wall accumulation, although growth remained near normal under mock conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Hypocotyl epidermal cells of dark-grown seedlings in imaging assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primary cell wall of elongating tissues, especially hypocotyl epidermis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key catalytic subunit of the cellulose synthase complex; terminal product-forming step for cellulose microfibril synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes UDP-glucose into beta-1,4-glucan chains that assemble into cellulose microfibrils at the plasma membrane
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering herbicide/CBI tolerance or probing cellulose biosynthesis; caution that altered CESA3 can affect wall deposition and mechanics.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane cellulose synthase complexes; Golgi-associated trafficking pools
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: KOR1; CESA complex components; cortical microtubule-associated guidance machinery
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS mutant screening; map-based cloning; complementation; cross-tolerance tests with cesa3 alleles; cellulose quantification; 13C-glucose incorporation assay; live-cell imaging; FRAP
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase catalytic subunit; glycosyltransferase family 2 with transmembrane domains and catalytic/gating regions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: P4B treatment; dark-grown seedlings; short-term 5 h and long-term 24 h assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural cell wall polymer enabling anisotropic growth and wall mechanics
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Primary cell wall cellulose synthase complex
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: P4B-sensitive CESA3 activity/function is required for normal cellulose synthesis and CSC secretion; specific CESA3 mutations confer resistance and preserve cellulose synthesis-related processes under P4B.
